definitions.text: All; every; qualifying a singular noun, indicating all examples of the thing so named seen as individual or separate items (compare every).
examples: Make sure you wash each bowl well.
The sun comes up each morning and sets each night.

definitions.text: Individually; separately; used in a sentence with a plural subject to indicate that the action or state described by the verb applies to all members of the described group individually, rather than collectively to the entire group.
examples: We ordered half a chicken each, but we each got a whole one.
You are each right in a different way.

definitions.text: Every one/thing individually or one by one.
examples: I'm going to give each of you a chance to win.
From each according to his ability, to each according to his needs.

definitions.text: An individual item: the least quantitative unit in a grouping.
